title: Newswire mobile breaking-news alerts, saved stories, and notification controls
productContext: Fictional global finance-news mobile app (product name 'Newswire') for iOS and Android. This surface combines three jobs on one part of the app: subscribing to breaking-news alerts (with topic, company, and severity controls), reading and managing saved stories offline, and tuning notifications to fight fatigue. Newswire is reader-and-aggregation software only; alert severities are an inferred relevance signal, not an editorial verdict or a guarantee of market impact, and any AI summary attached to an alert is machine-generated and source-attributed. The app is pre-scale: there is no published alert-volume metric, no accuracy figure, and no promise of real-time delivery. Every URL in this brief is synthetic and uses example.com.
users: alert subscriber who turns on breaking-news alerts for specific companies or topics and expects severity to be meaningful, not noise
offline reader who saves stories on cellular and reads them later on a plane or subway with no connection
notification-fatigued user who needs to mute, batch, or snooze alerts without losing the ability to get genuinely material developments
jobs: subscribe to and tune breaking-news alerts by company, topic, and severity, with a clear preview of how many alerts a setting will produce
save a story for offline reading and later find it, with its publisher identity, timestamp, and AI-summary attribution intact even when offline
fight notification fatigue by muting, batching, snoozing, or quiet-hours-ing alerts without the app claiming its severity is authoritative or its delivery is real-time
tell apart a publisher-authored alert headline from a machine-generated AI summary inside a notification
requiredJourneys: alert subscriber opens alert settings, picks a company and a severity threshold, sees a preview of expected alert volume, and confirms without being promised real-time delivery
offline reader saves a story while online, switches the device offline, and reopens the saved story with publisher, timestamp, and AI-summary label all intact
notification-fatigued user applies quiet hours and batched delivery, and confirms a genuinely material (highest-severity) development can still break through under the rules they set
any user receives a breaking-news notification and can tell at a glance whether the headline is publisher-authored or a machine-generated AI summary
constraints: Do not claim alerts are delivered in real time, instantly, or with guaranteed latency; the app is pre-scale and cannot promise delivery speed. Any timing language must be an expectation, not a guarantee, with a visible 'not guaranteed' treatment.

Alert severity is an inferred relevance signal, not an editorial verdict, a market-impact guarantee, or a source-of-truth ranking; do not display severity as an authoritative importance score, a confidence percentage, or a 'will move markets' promise.

Any AI summary attached to an alert or saved story must be labelled machine-generated and source-attributed; do not present it as the publisher's headline, an editorial verdict, or a source of record inside a notification or offline view.

Do not fabricate alert-volume counts, subscriber counts, accuracy figures, or delivery-speed metrics; any expected-volume preview must be clearly an estimate derived from the user's own settings, not a published benchmark.

Notification controls (mute, batch, snooze, quiet hours) must be reversible and individually legible: the user must be able to see what each control does and override it, and quiet hours must expose a 'material developments break through' exception that the user controls.

Offline saved stories must preserve publisher identity, timestamp, and AI-summary attribution; do not silently substitute a machine summary for a publisher article when offline, and surface an honest 'source not downloaded' state when the full article body is unavailable offline.
Exactly one primary action may lead any alert-subscription confirmation step; secondary actions (preview, skip, customize) must remain reachable without competing with it.

The product name Newswire is fictional; do not imply affiliation with any real news publisher, exchange, index provider, or rating agency, and do not imply any device-vendor or OS-level notification partnership.

mobileRules: the company, topic, and severity controls stay operable in a single-handed grip and the selected severity stays sticky while the volume preview updates
the volume-preview-visible state labels the estimate as derived from the user's settings, not a published benchmark
each saved story keeps its publisher label, timestamp, and AI-summary marker visible and readable with no connection
the source-not-downloaded state names honestly that the full publisher article body is unavailable offline and never silently swaps in the AI summary as a substitute
each control (mute, batch, snooze, quiet hours) shows its current effect inline and the material-breakthrough exception sits adjacent to quiet hours, not buried in a sub-screen
the all-muted state surfaces the material-breakthrough exception so a fatigued user can still opt to receive genuinely material developments
